articles.summary: A leading automobile manufacturer has launched a new electric vehicle model, featuring advanced technology and extended range. The company aims to expand its EV offerings.

articles.headline: Olympic qualifiers begin for various sports
articles.summary: Athletes across multiple sports have commenced Olympic qualifying events, vying for spots in the upcoming Games. Performances are being closely watched by national committees.

articles.headline: Cybersecurity firm detects sophisticated malware
articles.summary: A cybersecurity firm has identified a new and sophisticated malware strain targeting financial institutions globally. Experts are developing countermeasures to combat the threat.

articles.headline: Airfares rise sequentially since March on high demand and costs
articles.summary: International and domestic airfares in India have seen a sequential rise since March 2026. Data from travel agencies show international average booking values increased to ₹74,500 from ₹52,000 last year. Domestic fares averaged ₹8,450. The surge is driven by strong summer demand and higher operational costs, with significant jumps on routes like Bengaluru-Singapore and Mumbai-Kuala Lumpur.

articles.headline: Tata Steel expects demand softness in construction sector products
articles.summary: Tata Steel MD TV Narendran noted marginal softness in demand for long products used in real estate and infrastructure, partly due to labor shortages during elections. However, flat product demand remains strong, driven by the auto industry. The company expects steel prices to stay firm due to rising raw material costs and anticipates better Q1 margins compared to the previous quarter.

articles.headline: Indian refiners cut alkylate output impacting California fuel prices
articles.summary: Indian refiners have reduced alkylate production to prioritize domestic cooking gas (LPG) output amid West Asian supply disruptions. Alkylate is a vital motor fuel additive for California's clean-burning gasoline blend. The supply cut from Asia, combined with the near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz, is pushing gasoline prices toward $6 per gallon for California motorists.

articles.headline: IIT-Madras lab uses eye-tracking technology to support dyslexic children
articles.summary: The Language and Cognition Lab at IIT-Madras is using eye-tracking technology to study how children with dyslexia process language. By tracking pupil movement and blinking patterns, researchers aim to identify specific complex words that pose challenges. The goal is to create a repository of difficult terms and redesign study materials to improve diagnosis and learning outcomes in India.

articles.headline: New global test norms likely to raise Indian car prices
articles.summary: Starting April 2027, India will adopt the Worldwide Harmonised Light Vehicles Test Procedure (WLTP), replacing the current MIDC cycle. The shift, alongside CAFE III norms, could see certified mileage drop by 10-20% and vehicle prices rise by ₹30,000 to ₹2 lakh. Automakers will need to upgrade engine software and hardware to meet stricter real-world driving condition measurements.

articles.headline: PSBs and financial institutions to implement strict austerity measures
articles.summary: The Financial Services Department has instructed public sector banks, insurance firms, and financial institutions to implement cost-cutting measures amid the West Asia crisis. Steps include conducting meetings via video conferencing, limiting foreign travel for top executives, and adopting electric vehicles. Staff may also work from home up to two days per week to rationalize expenditure.

articles.headline: Supreme Court resolved to use video conferencing on miscellaneous days
articles.summary: To optimize fuel utilization and respond to austerity calls, Supreme Court judges have resolved to conduct hearings via video conferencing on miscellaneous days. The court also encouraged car-pooling among judges. Registry staff are permitted to work from home for up to two days a week, provided at least 50% of staff are available in the office to ensure uninterrupted functioning.

articles.headline: Delay in GST Council meeting flags concerns over structural reforms
articles.summary: The wait for the next GST Council meeting continues despite the formation of new state governments. Experts warn that prolonged gaps defer vital structural reforms and cause business uncertainty. Pending issues include rate rationalization for manufacturing and FMCG sectors, inverted duty structure corrections, and policy clarifications on interpretational issues monitoried by businesses.

articles.headline: Bars and coins drive 41% of India's gold demand
articles.summary: Demand for gold bars and coins reached 62.3 tonnes in the first quarter of 2026, accounting for 41% of India's total gold offtake. High domestic prices and a challenging equity market have boosted gold's appeal as an investment asset. In value terms, demand for bars and coins surged 129% to $10 billion, signaling a structural shift from jewelry to investment-led buying.

articles.headline: India develops world’s first AI-designed gene editor for crops
articles.summary: Scientists at the ICAR-Central Rice Research Institute have successfully validated the first AI-designed genome-editing tool for plants. Named Plant-OpenCRISPR1, it allows precise DNA tweaks for yield and disease resistance without foreign genes. This breakthrough offers a cheaper, more versatile alternative to standard CRISPR technology and could bypass existing global patent limitations.

articles.headline: Cognizant increases stock buyback target to $2 billion for 2026
articles.summary: Cognizant has authorized a $2 billion increase to its share repurchase program, bringing the total authorization to $3.45 billion. The company doubled its 2026 repurchase target to $2 billion, an increase of $1 billion over previous expectations. The additional repurchases are expected to be completed during the second quarter of 2026 as part of its capital allocation strategy.

articles.headline: Amazon Now to deploy 1,000 Eicher electric trucks by 2028
articles.summary: Amazon India plans to deploy 1,000 Volvo Eicher electric trucks to support its Amazon Now quick commerce service by 2028. Fifty trucks are already operational, covering 100-180 km daily between facilities. The initiative supports the company's sustainability goals, with the vehicles featuring a fast-charging time of approximately 50 minutes.

articles.headline: Zydus Wellness targets ₹5,700 crore revenue through strategic acquisitions
articles.summary: Zydus Wellness aims to reach ₹5,700 crore in revenue for FY27 as it integrates ₹7,800 crore worth of acquisitions, including Complan and Comfort Click. The company reported a 46.4% jump in sales for FY26. Management is focusing on transforming from a niche player into a broad health and nutrition platform, leveraging digital-first brands and international expansion for growth.

articles.headline: Vodafone Idea sees subscriber stabilization and profit boost
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea reported stabilized subscriber numbers at 192.8 million and a net profit aided by a one-time gain and restructured AGR liabilities. The company plans a ₹45,000 crore capex push over three years to expand 4G and 5G coverage. Revised AGR dues were finalized at ₹64,046 crore, providing more balance sheet clarity for the telecom operator's turnaround strategy.

articles.headline: GNFC reports 88% surge in Q4 PAT on lower costs
articles.summary: Gujarat Narmada Valley Fertilizers & Chemicals reported an 88% year-on-year jump in consolidated profit after tax to ₹396 crore for the March quarter. The result was driven by improved product realizations, lower raw material and energy-linked input costs, and one-time income in the fertilizer segment. Revenue from operations rose 7.4% to ₹2,208 crore.

articles.headline: Pearl Global to invest ₹250 crore to scale apparel capacity
articles.summary: Apparel exporter Pearl Global Industries plans to invest ₹200-250 crore in the current fiscal year to expand its global manufacturing footprint. The company, which serves brands like Gap and Zara, aims to become a ₹6,000-crore firm by 2028. It noted that demand from global retailers remains resilient despite geopolitical tensions and rising raw material costs.

articles.headline: Sanjay Dutt’s TIGERFIRE Vodka targets 25,000 sales in India
articles.summary: Premium spirit brand TIGERFIRE Vodka, co-founded by actor Sanjay Dutt, plans to expand to five Indian markets within a year. The company expects to sell up to 40,000 cases globally by year-end, with half targeted for India. Manufactured and bottled in France, the artisan-crafted vodka aims to double its presence over the next two years as part of its growth strategy.

articles.headline: JSW Steel eyes 80 million tonne capacity through joint ventures
articles.summary: JSW Steel is targeting an 80 million tonne annual capacity by 2030, supported by new joint ventures with JFE and POSCO. Despite near-term uncertainty due to the West Asia war, the company remains bullish on Indian growth. JSW Steel reported improved Q4 profits driven by high volumes and value-added product mix, with plans to fund future capex through internal accruals and debt.

articles.headline: Bangkok to host industry meet on last-mile logistics challenges
articles.summary: Industry leaders from the supply chain and commerce sectors will gather in Bangkok on May 21 for a two-day meet to discuss emerging challenges in last-mile logistics. The 2026 APAC edition aims to address the evolving needs of the logistics ecosystem, bringing together senior executives from retail and FMCG sectors across the region.

articles.headline: NTPC Group's installed power generation capacity surpasses 90 GW
articles.summary: The NTPC Group has achieved a milestone of 90 GW installed power generation capacity. This was reached following the successful trial of an 800 MW unit at the Patratu Vidyut Utpadan Nigam in Jharkhand. The milestone marks a significant step toward building an energy-secure future for India, according to the state-run power major.

articles.headline: EXIM Bank targets 10% credit growth in new fiscal year
articles.summary: The Export-Import Bank of India aims for a 10% jump in its loan book for the 2026-27 financial year, despite West Asia tensions. MD Harsha Bangari noted that while currency fluctuations impacted previous numbers, demand from exporters remains strong. The bank expects the share of rupee loans to rise further, even as geopolitical crises might put stress on some global supply chains.

articles.headline: India-New Zealand trade pact to boost generic drug exports
articles.summary: The new bilateral FTA between India and New Zealand provides zero-tariff access for all Indian products. Crucially, New Zealand will now recognize drug approvals from major regulators like the US and UK, significantly speeding up market entry for Indian generics and medical devices. The agreement also sets a target of $20 billion in FDI inflows from New Zealand into India over 15 years.

articles.headline: Beekeeping integration vital for climate-resilient agriculture productivity
articles.summary: Agriculture policy must move beyond seeds and fertilizers to recognize the role of bees in pollination. Research shows bee presence can improve yields in crops like mustard and sunflower by 15-45%. On World Bee Day, experts called for managed pollination services to be integrated into agricultural support frameworks to enhance crop productivity and restore natural ecological systems.

articles.headline: TVK's election win poses financial and economic tests for Tamil Nadu
articles.summary: The victory of Vijay’s TVK ends nearly 60 years of Dravidian duopoly, but its redistributive manifesto could balloon welfare spending to ₹1 lakh crore. Analysts worry the increased fiscal deficit might raise borrowing costs in the bond market. Additionally, a proposed 75% private sector job reservation for locals could create friction for Chennai's national talent-dependent IT sector.

articles.headline: Trump and Xi meeting provides few specifics on trade or Taiwan
articles.summary: The high-stakes meeting between US President Donald Trump and China’s Xi Jinping focused on trade and regional security but yielded few formal breakthroughs. Xi cautioned against the 'Thucydides Trap' and stated the Taiwan issue is non-negotiable. While Trump spoke of 'fantastic' deals for Boeing and US farmers, concrete details on tariffs and military hardware remain unclear.

articles.headline: Nifty recovers from day's low through short covering and FPI buying
articles.summary: The Indian stock market staged a dramatic intra-day recovery on Monday, with the Nifty 50 closing flat after initially losing 400 points. Recovery was fueled by short-covering and FPIs turning net buyers for ₹2,814 crore. IT stocks led the gains as a depreciating rupee boosted earnings prospects for exporters, despite lingering geopolitical concerns and high oil prices.

articles.headline: India-focused offshore funds see sharpest quarterly outflows since 2020
articles.summary: Offshore India-focused funds and ETFs witnessed net outflows of nearly $5 billion in the March quarter, the highest since the pandemic began. Morningstar reported that elevated valuations and a broad-based correction led to recalibration in market expectations. FIIs remained net sellers of nearly $14.2 billion during the quarter as geopolitical tensions hurt risk appetite.

articles.headline: Government explores options to revive stalled IDBI Bank privatization
articles.summary: Indian authorities are considering reducing the reserve price for IDBI Bank by up to 20% to revive buyer interest after previous efforts stalled. Bids were earlier halted because they fell short of minimum expectations or faced valuation hurdles. Officials intend to restart the process with interested bidders including Fairfax Financial, aiming for a price that captures the bank’s value.

articles.headline: Credit card transaction volume jumps 2.6 times in four years
articles.summary: A new RBI report shows that credit card transactions in India rose over 2.6 times between 2021 and 2025. Annual transaction value reached ₹23.2 lakh crore, driven by private sector bank dominance. Meanwhile, debit card usage fell as consumers shifted toward UPI and digital wallets. The report highlights the rapid 33-fold increase in digital payment volumes since 2016.

articles.headline: Silver to trade at premium as India imposes import curbs
articles.summary: Silver mutual funds and ETFs are expected to face supply shortages after the DGFT moved silver imports to the 'restricted' category. Importers now require valid government licenses. This move, combined with a recent import duty hike to 15%, is intended to reduce the current account deficit and encourage domestic refining, though it will likely lead to higher domestic silver premiums.

articles.headline: Agriculture Minister orders overhaul of ICAR's 113 research institutes
articles.summary: Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan has ordered a comprehensive revamp of the ICAR network following a surprise inspection of the National Research Centre for Grapes. Expressing dissatisfaction with accountability and results, the Minister called for institutions to be more farmer-oriented and commercially viable. A detailed report on revamping the apex research network is expected soon.

articles.headline: Energy firm SAEL to produce 1 GW from paddy stubble
articles.summary: Green energy firm SAEL plans to increase its power generation from biomass, specifically paddy stubble, to 1 gigawatt over five years. Using specialized Danish technology to burn stubble efficiently, the company operates 11 units in Punjab, Haryana, and Rajasthan. The initiative aims to reduce air pollution caused by stubble burning while expanding renewable energy capacity.

articles.headline: Hyderabad international airport commissions new 50,000 tpa cargo terminal
articles.summary: GMR Hyderabad International Airport has opened its second cargo terminal, engineered for long-term scalability. The 16,864 sq m facility will initially handle 50,000 tonnes per annum, with provisions to double capacity. The terminal is designed to improve operational efficiency and strengthen Hyderabad's position as a major regional logistics and cargo hub.

articles.headline: RBI imposes restrictions and withdrawal caps on Nagar Sahakari Bank
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has imposed supervisory restrictions on the Etawah-based Nagar Sahakari Bank for six months. Customers are now limited to withdrawing a maximum of ₹10,000 from their accounts. The directions, effective immediately, were issued due to concerns regarding the bank's financial stability and internal governance protocols.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh eyes strategic role in global nuclear economy
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh intends to position itself as a long-term partner in the emerging global nuclear economy, specifically focusing on small modular reactors (SMRs). IT Minister Nara Lokesh stated the state is building digital and energy infrastructure to support nuclear manufacturing and distributed supply chains, aiming for nearly ₹10 lakh crore in clean energy investments.

articles.headline: Enforcement Directorate attaches assets worth ₹700 crore of Mirchi aide
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has provisionally attached properties in Mumbai and Dubai valued at over ₹700 crore belonging to Iqbal Mirchi’s associate. The action, taken under the Fugitive Economic Offenders Act, targets assets linked to the late underworld don. The attached properties include prime real estate in Worli and various bank accounts tied to money laundering investigations.

articles.headline: India proposes medical aid to nations affected by Ebola outbreak
articles.summary: Former WHO Chief Scientist Soumya Swaminathan suggested India could support Uganda and the DRC with medical assistance and PPEs following the declaration of an Ebola public health emergency. While no cases have been reported in India, health authorities are reviewing screening and laboratory preparedness at ports and airports to prevent potential cross-border transmission.

articles.headline: Government warns internet providers over access to illegal betting sites
articles.summary: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has warned internet and VPN providers that allowing access to blocked prediction markets and betting platforms like Polymarket is illegal. A new law curbing online gambling went into effect on May 1. The ministry stated providers would face legal action if they continue to allow circumvention of domestic prohibitions.

articles.headline: TCS Rolls Out Average Salary Hikes of 6-8 Per Cent
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services has implemented average salary increases of 6-8% for its Indian employees, with top performers receiving hikes exceeding 10%. This marks a return to the company’s standard wage hike cycle after macroeconomic delays last year. Low-rated employees received 2-3% raises, while overseas staff saw increments of 2-4%. TCS had 584,519 staff as of March 2026.

articles.headline: Vodafone Idea Confident of Closing 35,000 Crore SBI-Led Loan Fast
articles.summary: Vodafone Idea (Vi) management is in deep engagement with an SBI-led consortium to close a ~35,000 crore funding round. This follows the Aditya Birla group's decision to infuse ~4,730 crore into the carrier. Vi plans a ~45,000 crore capex for 5G rollout over three years. Promoter holding is expected to rise to 28.5% post-conversion, while the government's share may dip slightly to 47.1%.

articles.headline: Bank Nifty Trades at Wide Discount Despite Strong Sector Earnings
articles.summary: The Bank Nifty is trading at a 43.5% discount to the Nifty 50, reflecting investor skepticism regarding the sustainability of current earnings momentum. While Bank Nifty's trailing EPS rose 172% over five years compared to 117% for the Nifty 50, its price-to-book ratio remains low at 1.83. Analysts cite rising bond yields and potential retail credit delinquencies as key headwinds.

articles.headline: India and Sweden Sign Six Pacts During Prime Minister Modi Visit
articles.summary: India and Sweden resolved to double bilateral trade in five years during PM Narendra Modi's visit. The nations elevated ties to a green strategic partnership, focusing on green steel, sustainable infrastructure, and maritime technology. Pacts include a virtual joint science center and an AI corridor. Modi also invited Nordic corporations to invest in India’s telecom and digital sectors.

articles.headline: RBI Governor Malhotra Monitors Supply Shocks for General Price Rise
articles.summary: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra stated the central bank is watching if West Asia conflict-induced supply shocks become embedded in the general price level. While currently maintaining a neutral stance, the RBI may warrant policy action if inflation generalized. The conflict has pushed crude oil prices above $100 per barrel and stressed India's balance of payments and the rupee.

articles.headline: Pakistan Conveys Revised Iranian Peace Plan to United States
articles.summary: Pakistan has shared a revised Iranian proposal with the US aimed at ending the West Asia war, though sources warn that narrowing differences remains difficult as goalposts keep shifting. A fragile ceasefire exists after US-Israeli strikes on Iran. Tehran seeks an end to the US blockade and compensation for damage, while Washington demands the dismantling of Iran's nuclear program.

articles.headline: Bondada Engineering Secures 470 Crore Order from Adani Group
articles.summary: Bondada Engineering has received multiple orders worth ~470 crore from the Adani Group for a 250 Mw Balance of System package. The company's stock closed at ~323.6, up 0.4% following the announcement of the fresh contracts.

articles.headline: Man Infraconstruction Acquires Sea-View Luxury Residential Development in Mumbai
articles.summary: Man Infraconstruction has announced the acquisition of a sea-view residential development project located off Bandstand in Mumbai. Shares of the company rose 1.2% to close at ~119.1 following the news of the luxury project addition to its portfolio.

articles.headline: Apollo Micro Systems Q4 Net Profit Surges Threefold to 38 Crore
articles.summary: Apollo Micro Systems reported that its net profit for Q4FY26 increased approximately three times year-on-year to reach ~38 crore. The company's stock responded positively to the results, surging 5.7% to close at ~311.1.

articles.headline: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Launches Vancomycin Hydrochloride Injection in US Market
articles.summary: Glenmark Pharmaceuticals has launched a generic version of the antibacterial prescription Vancomycin Hydrochloride for injection in the United States. The injection, available in 500 mg and 1 g vials, is bioequivalent to the reference listed drug. Shares of Glenmark rose 0.7% following the announcement.

articles.headline: Royal Enfield to Invest 2,500 Crore for Greenfield Facility in Andhra
articles.summary: Royal Enfield is set to acquire a land parcel in Tada, Andhra Pradesh, for a new manufacturing facility with a planned investment of ~2,500 crore. The expansion will be implemented in phases based on market demand. The motorcycle leader currently produces 1.46 million units annually and previously announced a ~958 crore expansion in Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: Tata Steel Targets Profitable and Sustainable Growth Over Market Share
articles.summary: Tata Steel MD T V Narendran stated that the company is prioritizing profitable and sustainable growth rather than just chasing overall market share. The company is facing environmental scrutiny in the Netherlands and rising input costs due to the West Asia conflict. While Q4 profit doubled, the company is cautious about UK turnaround and future environmental regulations in Europe.

articles.headline: TCPL Leverages AI to Augment Workforce and Capturing Micro Trends
articles.summary: Tata Consumer Products (TCPL) Chairman N Chandrasekaran informed shareholders that the company is using AI to capture micro trends and compress product development cycles. Alternative channels now contribute 41% of TCPL's India business. The company aims to build industry-specific AI solutions to serve evolving consumer needs for health and wellness.

articles.headline: Indian Hotel Investments Rose 67 Per Cent to 567 Million Dollars
articles.summary: India's hospitality sector saw hotel investments surge by 67% year-on-year in 2025, reaching $567 million across 28 transactions. According to JLL India, institutional capital and private equity firms accounted for 35% of the investment share, reflecting growing confidence in the country's tourism infrastructure.

articles.headline: Odisha Mining Corporation to Set Up 800 Crore Pellet Plant
articles.summary: The Odisha Mining Corporation (OMC) plans to invest ~800 crore in a 1.5 million tonne per annum pellet manufacturing plant in Keonjhar. The facility will utilize low-grade iron ore fines that are currently underused. The project marks OMC’s entry into downstream mineral processing and value-added manufacturing.

articles.headline: Prediction Market Platforms Polymarket and Kalshi Defy Indian Government Ban
articles.summary: Prediction market sites Polymarket and Kalshi continue to accept Indian users despite a Meity advisory labeling them illegal. Regulators are concerned about stablecoin payments and the circumvention of the Proga rules which ban online money games. Kalshi reportedly saw significant wagering volume from India on Indian Premier League cricket matches.

articles.headline: India Continues Russian Crude Purchases Despite Expiry of US Waiver
articles.summary: Indian refiners will continue to purchase crude oil from Russia after the US waiver for purchases loaded before April 17 expired on May 16. Officials stated that enough crude has been tied up to ensure domestic supply and that purchases are driven by commercial sense. Russian oil has been vital in maintaining fuel availability during the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: West Asia War Spikes Container Freight Rates Tenfold Since February
articles.summary: Shipping Ministry data shows transportation costs between India and West Asia have jumped nearly ten times since the conflict began in late February. While softening bunkering and insurance costs have provided some relief, charges at West Asian ports and subsequent transit costs remain high, severely impacting Indian container exporters.

articles.headline: Public Sector Banks Recall Staff to West Asia as Tensions Ease
articles.summary: Large Indian state-owned banks have started sending employees back to their overseas branches in the Gulf after temporarily recalling them during heighted conflict. Treasury and corporate banking operations were briefly handled from Mumbai as a precautionary step. Lenders stated there was no major disruption to business operations during the remote handling period.

articles.headline: India's Fertiliser Subsidy May Reach 2.41 Trillion for FY27
articles.summary: The national fertiliser subsidy bill for FY27 could surge by ~70,000 crore to ~2.41 trillion due to rising import costs of urea amid the West Asia crisis. Current stocks are reported to be comfortable at over 20 million tonnes, meeting 51% of requirements. The government is diversifying import routes away from the Strait of Hormuz to secure DAP and NPK supplies.

articles.headline: Stablecoins Fail Core Tests of Money While CBDC Offers Efficiency
articles.summary: The RBI stated in its payments report that stablecoins fail essential tests of money, including elasticity and integrity, posing jurisdictional risks. In contrast, Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) provides similar efficiencies without financial stability risks. UPI now accounts for 85.5% of total payment volume, with total retail payments growing 28% in late 2025.

articles.headline: RBI Decides Against Activating Countercyclical Capital Buffer for Banks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has decided not to activate the countercyclical capital buffer (CCyB) as prevailing economic conditions do not warrant it. The CCyB is a post-2008 reform designed to ensure banks build buffers during periods of high growth to use during stress. The RBI uses the credit-to-GDP gap as a primary indicator for this framework.

articles.headline: FCI to Raise 50,000 Crore Short-Term Loan for Foodgrain Operations
articles.summary: The Food Corporation of India is set to raise ~50,000 crore in short-term debt from scheduled banks to bridge cash flow mismatches in procurement and distribution. The tender includes a green shoe option for an additional ~25,000 crore. These unsecured loans will have a three-month tenure and are intended for welfare scheme foodgrain management.

articles.headline: Indian Government Considers Raising Edible Oil Import Duty to Aid Farmers
articles.summary: The government is examining a request from the vegetable oil industry to increase import duties to help domestic farmers secure better prices. India currently imports about 60% of its edible oil. The move comes as the government aims to curb foreign exchange outflows and reduce dependence on imported essential goods like oils and fertilisers.

articles.headline: India Set to Become Hyundai’s Second Largest Global Export Hub
articles.summary: Hyundai Motor India is on track to cross 1 million units of production capacity by FY28, making India its third largest production country and second largest export hub. The company has planned a ~7,500 crore capex for FY27, focusing on Chennai plant upgrades. Two new nameplate models will be introduced this financial year to boost volumes.

articles.headline: MSME Suppliers Urge Automakers to Share Rising West Asia Input Costs
articles.summary: The Federation of Indian Micro and Small & Medium Enterprises (Fisme) has asked SIAM and auto OEMs to absorb part of the wage and energy cost increases faced by suppliers due to the West Asia crisis. Fisme requested faster price revision approvals and expedited payment cycles through TReDS to manage supply chain stresses and ensure vendor sustainability.

articles.headline: Salesforce Used 12 Trillion AI Tokens Without Displacing Human Workers
articles.summary: Salesforce consumed over 12 trillion AI tokens last year for agentic use cases but maintains that technology will augment rather than replace humans. The firm shifted 3,000 employees from repetitive tasks to sales functions. Salesforce Chief Digital Evangelist Vala Afshar noted that token usage this year could surpass all of 2025 within two months due to rapid adoption.

articles.headline: Verlinvest Accelerates India Investments Amid Geopolitical and AI Anxiety
articles.summary: Belgian firm Verlinvest is increasing its India bets, betting that patient capital will outlast market volatility driven by West Asia conflict and AI-fueled job security fears. Managing Director Arjun Anand noted that brands offering convenience and value, particularly in quick commerce, are best positioned. Verlinvest currently holds stakes in nine Indian consumer brands.

articles.headline: Prudential Faces Distribution Challenges in Reviving Bharti AXA Life Insurance
articles.summary: Prudential plc faces hurdles in scaling Bharti AXA Life Insurance due to a limited distribution network and the absence of a strong banking partner. Prudential will acquire a 75% stake for ~3,500 crore, reducing its holding in ICICI Prudential to below 10%. Experts note that India’s insurance market is heavily dependent on bancassurance and extensive agency networks.

articles.headline: ICICI Bank Reaffirms Commitment to Majority Share in ICICI Prudential
articles.summary: ICICI Bank has stated its intention to retain majority ownership in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance. This reaffirmment follows news that its joint venture partner, Prudential plc, plans to reduce its stake to below 10% to facilitate its acquisition of Bharti Life Insurance. ICICI Bank remains the country’s third-largest private sector lender.

articles.headline: Parliament Panel to Review Groundwork for Viksit Bharat Guarantee Rollout
articles.summary: The Parliamentary Standing Committee on Rural Development will meet on May 20 to review preparations for the implementation of the Viksit Bharat Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission-Gramin (VB-G RAM G). The new act will replace MGNREGA across India starting July 1, 2026. Officials will brief the panel on mission preparatory work.

articles.headline: India to Host Quad Foreign Ministers Meeting Next Week in Delhi
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar will host Quad foreign ministers from the US, Australia, and Japan in New Delhi on May 26. The meeting will address global challenges including the West Asia crisis and Indo-Pacific security. Attendees include US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong.

articles.headline: Home Ministry Amends Citizenship Rules for Afghan, Bangladeshi, and Pakistani Nationals
articles.summary: The Home Ministry has introduced new requirements under the Citizenship Rules, 2009, mandating that applicants for Indian citizenship declare the status of their native passports. Applicants must surrender valid or expired passports from Pakistan, Afghanistan, or Bangladesh within 15 days of approval. The move follows the 2019 Citizenship Amendment Act framework.

articles.headline: Evergrande Liquidators Seek 8.4 Billion Dollars from PwC in Hong Kong
articles.summary: Liquidators for the collapsed Chinese property giant Evergrande Group are seeking $8.4 billion in damages from PwC, alleging negligence in auditing. This follows massive fines already imposed on the auditor by Chinese and Hong Kong authorities. Evergrande collapsed with more than $300 billion in liabilities, triggering a major crisis in China's real estate sector.

articles.headline: Baidu Quarterly Revenue Beats Estimates Driven by AI Cloud Growth
articles.summary: China’s search giant Baidu surpassed market estimates for quarterly revenue as demand for its AI-powered cloud services offset weakness in its advertising business. Revenue from its AI-core business, which includes robotaxis and cloud units, jumped 49% to $2 billion. CEO Robin Li stated that AI has become the primary growth driver for the company.

articles.headline: IMF Upgrades UK Growth Outlook Despite Domestic Political Turmoil Warnings
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund has raised Britain's 2026 growth forecast to 1.0%, up from a previous estimate of 0.8%. However, the IMF warned that domestic political instability and the war in the Middle East could dampen near-term prospects and hit investment. The Fund noted the UK economy has remained resilient but faces significant domestic uncertainty.

articles.headline: Russian Drones Hit Chinese-Owned Cargo Vessel in Black Sea Port
articles.summary: Ukrainian authorities reported that Russian drones hit a Chinese-owned civilian cargo ship, the Ksl Deyang, near Odesa. The ship was sailing under a Marshall Islands flag with a Chinese crew. President Volodymyr Zelenskiy confirmed the strike on X, which damaged two civilian vessels. This marks a significant incident involving Chinese commercial property in the conflict zone.

articles.headline: Trump Approval Hits Second-Term Low Amid Growing West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: A New York Times/Siena poll found US President Donald Trump's approval rating has sunk to 37%, a second-term low. Nearly two-thirds of voters believe going to war with Iran was the wrong decision. Concerns over the cost of living and the economy dominate voter sentiment, with 64% disapproving of Trump's economic handling as he heads into midterm elections.

articles.headline: Trump Administration Immigration Crackdown Separated Over 100,000 Families
articles.summary: A Brookings Institution analysis suggests that over 100,000 children have been separated from their parents during the current US immigration crackdown. Roughly three-quarters of these children are US citizens. The report argues that official statistics from the Department of Homeland Security significantly undercount the total number of family separations.

articles.headline: Elon Musk Denies Plans to Sell SpaceX Shares Before IPO
articles.summary: Billionaire Elon Musk stated he is not selling any SpaceX shares as the company prepares for a potential initial public offering as soon as next week. SpaceX shareholders were notified of a 5-for-1 stock split to reduce share prices for new investors. The company is seeking a valuation of more than $2 trillion, which would make it the largest IPO of all time.

articles.headline: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris to Contain Iran War Fallout
articles.summary: Finance officials from the G7 nations convened in Paris to devise a plan to stabilize energy markets and global inflation caused by the US-Israeli war with Iran. Discussions focused on the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and Export restrictions on minerals. The meeting also included representatives from India and Brazil to coordinate global economic responses.

articles.headline: WHO Declares Ebola in DRC and Uganda a Public Health Emergency
articles.summary: The World Health Organization has declared the recent Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern. At least 88 deaths have been recorded. The Bundibugyo strain is proving dangerous as existing vaccines and tests are ineffective against it. Global health officials are concerned about high regional transmission.

articles.headline: BSE CEO Credits Strategy and Sharp Product Design for Derivatives Push
articles.summary: BSE MD & CEO Sundararaman Ramamurthy stated that the exchange’s derivatives success is driven by sharp product design and a Thursday expiry strategy. Individual investors now contribute 30% of Sensex derivatives volumes. The exchange is seeking approvals for three new monthly index derivatives and focusing on responsible participation and long-term liquidity beyond weekly expiries.

articles.headline: BSE Could Replace Wipro in Nifty 50 Index This September
articles.summary: Analysts at Quiddity Advisors expect BSE Ltd to replace Wipro in the benchmark Nifty 50 index during the September 2026 rebalancing. BSE satisfies inclusion criteria as its float market capitalization is over 1.5 times that of Wipro. The inclusion could lead to passive inflows of ~$639 million from funds tracking the flagship index.

articles.headline: Sensex Ends Flat After Plunging 1,000 Points on West Asia Tension
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices erased early losses of over 1,000 points to end largely unchanged on Monday. Gains in IT and pharma stocks, along with FPI buying of ~2,813 crore, supported the recovery. Sentiment remains fragile due to soaring crude prices and the US-Iran conflict, though reports of a potential sanctions waiver on Iranian oil provided late-day optimism.

articles.headline: Mutual Funds Rotate Into Banking Stocks While Trimming IT and Metals
articles.summary: Indian mutual funds rebalanced their portfolios in April, with ICICI Bank, SBI, and Kotak Mahindra Bank emerging as the most bought stocks. Fund managers collectively invested ~15,000 crore into these three banks as valuations dipped. Conversely, Wipro and HDFC Bank saw the highest selling, with investors pulling out around ~4,600 crore from the two stocks.

articles.headline: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey Issues Formal Advisory on AI Risks
articles.summary: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey warned that AI tools like Claude Mythos could exploit market vulnerabilities faster than traditional tools. He noted that while India’s financial ecosystem is resilient, volatility has increased due to global supply shocks. Sebi has established an incident response framework and requires all algorithmic trading to be simulated before deployment.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Rules 2016 Benami Law Provisions Apply Retrospectively
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has clarified that procedural provisions of the 2016 Benami Act amendments, including property attachment and confiscation, can apply to transactions made before 2016. While stricter penal provisions cannot be applied retroactively, the ruling allows authorities to scrutinize old family property arrangements where the registered owner differs from the actual funder.

articles.headline: Real Estate Developers Save Hundreds of Crores via AI Adoption
articles.summary: Indian realty firms like Godrej Properties and M3M are using AI to optimize floor space and compress design cycles. Godrej reported savings of nearly ~400 crore on a single project through AI-led excavation and construction reduction. M3M completed a project 136 days ahead of schedule, saving ~180 crore. AI sales assistance now accounts for up to 45% of total real estate sales.

articles.headline: Apple and Google Raise Hardware Concerns Over India’s D2D Ambitions
articles.summary: Tech giants Apple and Google have highlighted hardware limitations and the need for global standards regarding India's direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity plans. Full-scale D2D for voice and data requires deep hardware changes and spectrum coordination. Stakeholders suggest focusing on easier-to-support satellite SOS features under existing global frameworks.

articles.headline: Air India Flights Delayed at Mumbai Due to Ground Staff Strike
articles.summary: Operational disruptions hit Air India and Air India Express at Mumbai airport after ground staff from agency AIASL staged a protest for higher wages. AIASL caters to 80 domestic and foreign airlines. Employees resumed work after management assured that their demands would be reviewed. Approximately 650 flights per day are handled by the agency across India.

articles.headline: Supreme Court's Reservations on UAPA Bail Judgments Highlight Liberty
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed 'serious reservations' about previous judgments denying bail under UAPA to activists like Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam. The court stressed that indefinite incarceration due to a low prima facie threshold for bail is unacceptable. It reaffirmed that personal liberty and speedy trials are fundamental rights, even under anti-terror laws, and noted low conviction rates in UAPA cases.

articles.headline: India, Norway Elevate Ties to Green Strategic Partnership
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Norwegian PM Jonas Gahr Støre agreed to upgrade bilateral relations to a Green Strategic Partnership. Discussions focused on geopolitical issues, a rules-based order, and diplomacy. The partnership aims to leverage India's scale and Norway's technology for green energy and innovation. Agreements were signed on health, space, and digitalization, with India inviting Norwegian investment in its 500 GW clean energy target by 2030.

articles.headline: Finance Ministry Mandates Austerity Measures for Public Sector Banks
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry has directed public sector banks and insurers to implement strict austerity measures, including reducing travel costs and adopting electric vehicles. Meetings should increasingly be held via videoconferencing. This initiative aligns with Prime Minister Modi's call for national austerity to rationalize expenditure amid global economic uncertainty and promote domestic tourism.

articles.headline: Tourist Trampled to Death by Fighting Elephants at Karnataka Camp
articles.summary: A 33-year-old tourist from Chennai died at the Dubare Elephant Camp in Kodagu, Karnataka, after being caught between two captive elephants, Kanjan and Marthanda, during a clash. The State government has ordered an inquiry and a safety review for all elephant tourism centers and camps, establishing new standard operating procedures.

articles.headline: V.D. Satheesan Sworn In as Kerala CM; UDF Forms Government
articles.summary: Congress leader V.D. Satheesan took office as Kerala's Chief Minister, leading a 20-member cabinet for the United Democratic Front (UDF) after its landslide victory. The cabinet immediately approved welfare measures, including free bus travel for women and a ₹3,000 monthly pension hike. Other initiatives include college stipends for girls and increased honorariums for ASHA workers.

articles.headline: Coaching Centre Owner Arrested in NEET-UG Paper Leak Case
articles.summary: The CBI arrested Shivaraj Motegaonkar, owner of Renukai Chemistry Classes in Latur, in connection with the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. He is the tenth arrest in the case, accused of having access to exam questions days prior. Investigators recovered a chemistry question bank identical to the leaked paper.

articles.headline: BCCI Not a Public Authority Under RTI Act, Rules Central Information Commission
articles.summary: The Central Information Commission (CIC) ruled that the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) is not a 'public authority' under the Right to Information (RTI) Act. The CIC determined the BCCI is an autonomous private body, not substantially financed or controlled by the government, overturning previous classifications and upholding its private status.

articles.headline: US Prosecutors Drop Charges Against Gautam Adani
articles.summary: US federal prosecutors requested dismissal of criminal fraud and conspiracy charges against billionaire Gautam Adani and co-defendants. The move cited 'prosecutorial discretion'. Separately, Adani Enterprises agreed to pay $275 million to the US Treasury to settle civil charges for alleged sanction violations related to Iranian LPG shipments.

articles.headline: New CAA Rules: Applicants Must Surrender Foreign Passports
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry has amended Citizenship Rules under the CAA, requiring applicants from Pakistan, Afghanistan, and Bangladesh to surrender their foreign passports within 15 days of citizenship approval. Applicants must also disclose entry details, with penalties for false information, to streamline the process for non-Muslim migrants.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low of 96.36 Against Dollar Amid Global Tensions
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to a record closing low of 96.36 against the US dollar, influenced by rising global oil prices and US Treasury yields. Increased dollar demand from oil importers and foreign institutional investor selling pressured the rupee. The RBI intervened to manage volatility, while the 10-year benchmark bond yield also rose.

articles.headline: India to Continue Russian Oil Purchases Based on Commercial Viability
articles.summary: India will continue purchasing Russian crude oil if it remains commercially viable, despite the expiration of US waivers. Imports of Russian oil surged significantly in May. The government prioritizes securing stable energy supplies for its growing economy, noting Russia's pricing and logistical advantages.

articles.headline: Iran Establishes Authority to Regulate Strait of Hormuz Transit
articles.summary: Iran has formed the Persian Gulf Strait Authority to assert control over maritime transit through the Strait of Hormuz. The new body will manage operations, security, and provide real-time updates for ships passing through the vital waterway, which handles a significant portion of global oil and gas shipments.

articles.headline: Sunrisers Hyderabad, Gujarat Titans Secure IPL Playoff Spots
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Chennai Super Kings to secure an IPL playoff berth, also confirming Gujarat Titans' qualification. Heinrich Klaasen and Ishan Kishan were instrumental in SRH's chase of 181. CSK's playoff hopes are now uncertain.

articles.headline: Aaron Rai Wins PGA Championship, First Major Title
articles.summary: Englishman Aaron Rai secured his maiden Major title by winning the PGA Championship with a final-round 65, finishing nine-under par. He becomes the first Englishman since 1919 to win the event, securing a $3.69 million prize and rising to world No. 15.

articles.headline: VD Satheesan Sworn In as Kerala CM; UDF Forms Government
articles.summary: Congress leader VD Satheesan took office as the 13th Kerala Chief Minister, leading a 21-member UDF cabinet. Immediate welfare measures included free bus rides for women, increased ASHA worker honorariums, and a new department for senior citizens, marking the UDF's return after a decade.

articles.headline: US Jury Rules Against Elon Musk in OpenAI Mission Lawsuit
articles.summary: A US jury ruled that OpenAI is not liable for abandoning its original nonprofit mission to benefit humanity, finding that Elon Musk brought his lawsuit too late. The verdict ends a high-profile legal battle over the commercialization of artificial intelligence.

articles.headline: Rajnath Singh Visits Vietnam and South Korea to Boost Defense Ties
articles.summary: Defense Minister Rajnath Singh began a four-day trip to Vietnam and South Korea to enhance strategic military cooperation, maritime partnerships, and defense industrial collaborations. The visit aims to promote stability in the Indo-Pacific region and includes discussions on potential BrahMos missile deals and next-generation defense systems.

articles.headline: Iran Launches Bitcoin-Backed Insurance for Strait of Hormuz Shipping
articles.summary: Iran introduced 'Hormuz Safe,' a Bitcoin-settled insurance service for vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz. This initiative aims to formalize maritime tolls and generate revenue amid a US naval blockade, allowing for cryptographically verifiable policies to bypass traditional financial systems.

articles.headline: SEBI Chief: Indian Markets Resilient Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: SEBI Chairperson Tuhin Kanta Pandey stated Indian capital markets remain resilient despite geopolitical turmoil and FII selling. Market capitalization has grown, and retail participation has surged, providing a buffer. SEBI is also addressing AI cybersecurity threats and influencer registration.

articles.headline: PM Modi Advocates Diplomacy for Global Conflict Resolution
articles.summary: During his visit to Norway, Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasized that military action alone cannot resolve conflicts like those in West Asia and Ukraine, advocating for dialogue and diplomacy. India and Norway also signed roadmaps for cooperation in AI and critical minerals.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Orders Apple to Cooperate in Antitrust Probe
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court directed Apple to cooperate with the Competition Commission of India (CCI) in an antitrust investigation into the iPhone app market. The court prevented the CCI from passing a final order before July 15, while allowing the probe to continue despite Apple's challenge to penalty laws.

articles.headline: Bharti Airtel Briefly Overtakes HDFC Bank as India's Second Most Valuable Firm
articles.summary: Bharti Airtel's market capitalization briefly surpassed HDFC Bank's, making it India's second-most valuable listed company. Driven by strong earnings, Airtel's valuation reached ~11.9 lakh crore intraday, though HDFC Bank later regained the position. Reliance Industries remains the market leader.

articles.headline: Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in Indian Insurance Venture to 74%
articles.summary: Liberty Mutual Insurance increased its stake in Liberty General Insurance to 74%, following regulatory changes allowing higher foreign investment. This expansion reflects growing global interest in India's under-penetrated insurance market, with Liberty's gross written premiums reaching ₹2,814 crore in FY26.

articles.headline: JSW Energy Sells 1.02% Stake in JSW Steel for ₹3,150 Crore
articles.summary: JSW Energy divested 2.5 crore shares of JSW Steel for approximately ₹3,150 crore through block deals. Investors including GQG Partners participated. Following the sale, JSW Energy's remaining stake in JSW Steel is around 1.84%.

articles.headline: Commerce Minister Urges Import Substitution for Export Growth
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal urged Indian manufacturers to identify import substitution opportunities and reduce reliance on foreign goods, especially capital goods. This is crucial for achieving the national export target of $1 trillion and strengthening domestic industry by promoting Indian-made products.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Seeks Extension for Interim Chairman Keki Mistry
articles.summary: HDFC Bank plans to request a three-month extension from the RBI for interim chairman Keki Mistry, prioritizing board continuity while awaiting a legal review before appointing a permanent successor.

articles.headline: TVS Venu Group to Acquire Stake in Jana Small Finance Bank
articles.summary: The TVS Venu Group will acquire up to a 9.9% stake in Jana Small Finance Bank through warrants and secondary purchases, with TVS Motor Company taking a 4.9% share. This investment aims to bolster the bank's capital adequacy and support India's digital financial ecosystem.

articles.headline: Adani Enterprises Settles US Iran Sanctions Case for $275 Million
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises agreed to a $275 million settlement with the US Treasury's OFAC over alleged violations involving Iranian LPG imports between 2023-2025. The settlement, which does not admit guilt, followed the company's cooperation and implementation of stronger compliance measures.

articles.headline: Strides Pharma Reports 51% Jump in Q4 Net Profit
articles.summary: Strides Pharma Science posted a consolidated net profit of ₹129.28 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, a 51% increase year-on-year, driven by strong performance in key markets outside the US.

articles.headline: Maruti Suzuki Commences Production at Second Kharkhoda Facility
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki India has started production at its second plant in Kharkhoda, Haryana, adding 250,000 units of annual capacity, bringing total capacity to 2.65 million units. The company plans further investments to expand capacity across multiple states.

articles.headline: RBI Promotes Gunveer Singh to Executive Director
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has promoted Gunveer Singh to Executive Director, effective May 18, 2026. Singh, with over 30 years of experience, will oversee the Department of Payment and Settlement Systems, previously serving as its Chief General Manager-in-Charge.

articles.headline: IEA Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Draining Rapidly
articles.summary: IEA chief Fatih Birol warned that commercial oil stocks are depleting at a record pace due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closures. Global inventories dropped significantly in March-April, and rising seasonal demand is expected to further strain supply, despite strategic reserve releases.

articles.headline: Bank of India Hikes Fixed Deposit Rates on Select Tenures
articles.summary: Bank of India has increased interest rates on fixed deposits under ₹3 crore, offering up to 6.7% for a three-year tenure. Senior citizens will receive additional benefits. The move aims to attract more deposits amid evolving market conditions.

articles.headline: RBI Removes Investment Fluctuation Reserve for Certain Banks
articles.summary: The RBI has removed the Investment Fluctuation Reserve (IFR) requirement for banks maintaining capital for market risk under a revised framework. Existing IFR balances can now be transferred to statutory reserves, boosting Tier 1 capital for these institutions.
